question: What material specifications from Table 5.2-4 are used for RPV construction, and what does the text say about their qualification?

nuscale_ch05_0009.title: Overpressure Protection
nuscale_ch05_0009.summary: This section outlines NuScale's compliance with overpressure protection requirements by referencing applicable ASME Boiler and Pressure Vessel Code (BPVC) and Operations and Maintenance (OM) code cases, as mandated by various sections of 10 CFR 50.55a and related Regulatory Guides (RGs). It details how code cases for design, fabrication, construction, preservice, and inservice inspections and testing are selected and verified to meet NRC-endorsed standards, with a consolidated list provided in Table 5.2-1.

nuscale_ch05_0015.title: Reactor Vessel Materials
nuscale_ch05_0015.summary: The Reactor Vessel section describes the NuScale Power Module’s reactor pressure vessel (RPV), which houses the reactor core, steam generators, and pressurizer within a containment vessel. The RPV is a critical pressure-retaining component designed to prevent the release of fission products, and its materials are selected and fabricated according to stringent ASME Boiler and Pressure Vessel Code standards to ensure integrity throughout the plant’s design lifetime.

nuscale_ch05_0010.title: Reactor Coolant Pressure Boundary Materials
nuscale_ch05_0010.summary: The Reactor Coolant Pressure Boundary (RCPB) materials, including welds, are selected and fabricated in accordance with ASME BPVC standards and meet the regulatory requirements of 10 CFR 50, including General Design Criteria for quality, environmental compatibility, and fracture prevention. The materials used for Class 1 components, such as the reactor pressure vessel (RPV) and steam generators (SGs), are specified in Table 5.2-4, ensuring compliance with all relevant material and testing standards.

nuscale_ch05_0013.title: References
nuscale_ch05_0013.summary: This section lists references and standards used in the NuScale Final Safety Analysis Report regarding the integrity of the reactor coolant boundary. It includes guidelines and reports from the Electric Power Research Institute and the American Society of Mechanical Engineers, as well as tables summarizing relevant ASME code cases for materials and components used in nuclear facility applications.

nuscale_ch05_0011.title: Reactor Coolant Pressure Boundary Inservice Inspection and Testing
nuscale_ch05_0011.summary: This section describes the materials and standards used for threaded fasteners in key reactor coolant system components, emphasizing the use of Alloy 718 for its compatibility and resistance to stress corrosion cracking. It also outlines the requirements for preservice and inservice inspection and testing of reactor coolant pressure boundary components, specifying adherence to the latest ASME Boiler and Pressure Vessel Code (BPVC) editions as mandated by regulatory requirements.

nuscale_ch05_0014.title: Reactor Vessel
nuscale_ch05_0014.summary: The Reactor Vessel section describes the NuScale Power Module’s reactor pressure vessel (RPV), which houses the reactor core, steam generators, and pressurizer within a single vessel surrounded by a containment vessel. The RPV is a critical pressure-retaining component that forms part of the reactor coolant pressure boundary, supporting various internal systems and ensuring the containment of fission products. Materials for the RPV are carefully selected and fabricated according to ASME Boiler and Pressure Vessel Code standards to maintain integrity throughout the plant’s design lifetime.

nuscale_ch05_0025.title: References
nuscale_ch05_0025.summary: This section details the materials used in the construction of the pressurizer and its components within the NuScale reactor coolant system. It specifies the types of steel and alloys used for various parts, their compliance with relevant BPVC material specifications, and highlights corrosion protection measures such as weld cladding with stainless steel and nickel-based alloys.

nuscale_ch05_0017.title: Reactor Vessel Integrity
nuscale_ch05_0017.summary: This section outlines the procedures and regulatory compliance measures to ensure the integrity of the NuScale reactor vessel throughout its 60-year design life. It details the development of operating procedures to prevent exceeding pressure-temperature limits, evaluates the vessel’s resistance to pressurized thermal shock and upper-shelf energy degradation, and confirms that all criteria remain within regulatory limits at end-of-life fluence levels.

edges.description: Section A references the applicable ASME BPVC code cases and regulatory requirements for design, fabrication, and construction, which are prerequisites for the material specifications and qualifications discussed in Section B regarding the RPV.

Section A references the use of ASME BPVC code cases for design, fabrication, and construction, while Section B specifies that RCPB (including RPV) materials conform to ASME BPVC Section III and II requirements, thus Section B provides the specific material compliance details that Section A generically references.

Section B states that the materials used for the reactor coolant pressure boundary, including those for the RPV, must comply with ASME BPVC and regulatory requirements, while Section A identifies the RPV as a key component constructed from these materials, thus linking the general qualification requirements in B to the specific application in A.

Section A describes the reactor pressure vessel (RPV) and its role in the reactor coolant system, while Section B lists references including standards and guidelines (such as ASME NQA-1) that are relevant for qualifying the materials used in RPV construction, thus connecting the material specifications to their qualification basis.

Section A describes the material specifications and qualification standards for RPV construction, while Section B lists references that support or provide the basis for these standards, indicating that the standards in A are supported by the documents cited in B.

Section A specifies that threaded fastener materials for the RPV conform to ASME BPVC Sections II and III, while Section B discusses compliance with ASME BPVC Section III code cases for design, fabrication, and construction, connecting both sections through the use of ASME BPVC standards for RPV material qualification.
Section A specifies the material (nickel-based Alloy 718) and its qualification (conformance to ASME BPVC) for RPV threaded fasteners, which are part of the RPV construction described in Section B.

Section A specifies that threaded fasteners for the RPV use Alloy 718 and conform to ASME BPVC Sections II and III, which supports Section B's general statement that all RCPB materials, including those for the RPV, comply with ASME BPVC Sections II and III requirements.

Section A describes the RPV as a pressure-retaining component of the reactor coolant system, while Section B outlines the regulatory codes (ASME BPVC and 10 CFR 50.55a) that govern the design, fabrication, and construction of such components, thus connecting the material specifications in Table 5.2-4 to their required qualification standards.

Section A provides a general description of the reactor pressure vessel (RPV) and its role in the reactor system, while Section B focuses specifically on the materials used for the RPV, which are detailed in Table 5.2-4 and discussed in terms of their qualification.

Section B specifies that the RPV materials must comply with ASME BPVC and regulatory requirements, and refers to Section 5.3 (Section A) for details, indicating that Section A contains the specific material specifications and their qualification status as required by Section B.

Section A identifies the specific RPV components and refers to Section 5.2 for their material specifications, while Section B discusses the regulatory compliance and code cases (ASME BPVC, 10 CFR 50.55a) that govern the qualification of those materials, thus connecting the material choices to their regulatory qualification.

Section A refers to Section 5.2 for the material specifications of the RPV upper shell and upper head, which are detailed in Section B, thereby connecting the pressurizer material discussion to the broader RPV material qualifications.

Section A references Section B by directing the reader to Section 5.2 for material descriptions of RPV components, while Section B provides the regulatory and code compliance details for those materials, connecting the specific material selections to their qualification requirements.

Section A specifies the material (nickel-based Alloy 718) and qualification standards (ASME BPVC Sections II and III) for RPV threaded fasteners, which are components described in Section B as part of the RPV construction.
Section B references Section 5.2 for the material specifications of the RPV upper shell and upper head, which are the subject of the qualification discussion in Section A.
Section B references Section 5.2 for the material specifications of the RPV upper shell and upper head, which are the same specifications discussed in Section A regarding RPV construction.

Section A discusses the development of operating procedures to ensure compliance with technical specifications for reactor vessel integrity, while Section B outlines compliance with ASME BPVC code cases and regulatory requirements for the integrity of the reactor coolant boundary, both connecting through the need to meet material and procedural standards for RPV construction and qualification.

Section A discusses the development of operating procedures to ensure compliance with technical specifications for reactor vessel integrity, while Section B describes the physical components and materials of the reactor pressure vessel (RPV), connecting them through the need for procedures (A) that ensure the materials and design described in B are operated within safe limits.

Section B specifies that the RPV materials must comply with ASME BPVC and regulatory requirements, while Section A refers to procedures ensuring operational compliance but does not directly address material specifications or their qualification, thus Section B provides the material qualification context relevant to the analysis question.
